Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria), mild hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, and glucosuria with ketonuria, suggesting possible acute or chronic kidney disease with impaired glucose handling. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate urinary tract pathology. Electrolytes are mostly within range except for mild hyperchloremia. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.
